benchmark.name: Causally Grounded Finite Agents Benchmark
benchmark.charter: Evaluate whether finite agents succeed for the right causal reasons by requiring behavior plus suite-specific structure and anti-cheat gates.

suite.name: Suite C: Re-Engagement Under World Change

run.command: Synthesis of committed Paper 22, Paper 23A, Paper 23B, and July 6 Paper 23A Modal report; Paper 23B needs a first-class JSONL runner.

minimum_pass_rule.notes: Suite C is a bounded positive with a recovery gap: re-engagement and no-false-calm structure gates pass, but strict recovery and public JSONL cost-normalized scoring remain partial.

gates.name: silence_replication
gates.threshold: baseline learned current-replay probe has near-zero affected-bucket probes after shift
gates.value: 0.0 affected post-shift nulls in the July 6 Modal report

gates.name: reengagement
gates.threshold: candidate restores affected post-shift probe density and selects affected over unaffected buckets
gates.value: Paper 23A headline reaches 137% pre-shift density and 3.04x unaffected buckets

gates.name: recovery
gates.threshold: candidate reaches strict MAE threshold or justified oracle-relative recovery in most seeds
gates.value: decision-layer variants recover 1/3 to 2/3 seeds; oracle_source recovers 3/3

gates.name: no_false_calm
gates.threshold: probe-rate drop must pair with attribution or outcome improvement
gates.value: fixed_surprise_decrement has low AUC but 0/3 recovery

gates.name: cost_aware_inquiry
gates.threshold: lower inquiry cost than scheduled or always-probe controls at comparable recovery
gates.value: decision-layer variants reduce post-shift AUC by 37-46%, but public JSONL cost-normalized scorer is not emitted yet

gates.name: second_shift_reopenability
gates.threshold: affected-bucket probes rise again after a second shift
gates.value: 2.05x leaky effort, 1.81x decision refractory, 2.18x burst then refractory

baselines.name: p22_learned_current_replay
baselines.result: self-silences after shift; July 6 report baseline affected post-shift nulls are 0.0

baselines.name: p23a_surprise_no_cooling
baselines.result: re-engages but over-fires; 0/3 recoveries and high post-shift AUC

baselines.name: fixed_surprise_decrement
baselines.result: false calm negative control; low AUC but 0/3 recovery

allowed_claim: Diagnostic benchmark packaging for re-engagement under world change, with positive evidence that decision-layer cooling preserves re-openability while no-false-calm controls reject signal-layer surprise erasure.
non_claims: Not a terminal Suite C pass.
Not a human or neural habituation result.
